reasoning:
For Alireza Firouzja to register an Elo rating at least 1% higher than his April 2025 rating of 2757, he needs to reach an official FIDE rating of 2785. The FIDE rating lists are exclusively updated on the first of each month, which Wikipedia subsequently mirrors.
1. 2025-04-20: The official FIDE rating will not update between April 1st and May 1st. His rating will certainly remain 2757. (p=0.02, the minimum bound)
2. 2025-05-13: The May list incorporates games played in April. Firouzja's reported commitments in April (such as the Paris Freestyle Chess event) are Chess960 and do not count for classical FIDE ratings. He simply won't have the classical rated games necessary to gain 28 points by May. (p=0.03)
3. 2025-07-12: By July, Firouzja will likely have played the Superbet Chess Classic Romania (May 5-17) and potentially other events. To gain 28 points, he would need an extraordinary performance (e.g., scoring +4 or +5 against 2750+ opposition) across these sparse events. While he has hit 2800 before, sustaining such a run over 1-2 tournaments is rare. (p=0.08)
4. 2025-10-10: By October, adding tournaments like the Sinquefield Cup gives him a longer horizon to string together classical victories and hit the required 2785. The base rate for a top-10 player seeing a 30-point streak up to the 2780s within half a year is maybe 10-15%. Given his undeniable talent and past peak of 2804, a hot streak is possible but still unlikely. (p=0.14)
